title: NASA Finds a Colorful Puzzle Inside Asteroid Bennu
summary: Scientists from NASA studied a special rock from an asteroid called Bennu. They found that the rock is not the same all over. Instead, it has three different types of tiny spots with different chemicals. One spot has simple carbon stuff, another has minerals that form with water, and a third has nitrogen, which is important for life. This shows that water on Bennu long ago did not mix things evenly. The samples came from the OSIRIS-REx mission, which brought them to Earth safely. This helps us learn about how the solar system started and what ingredients for life might be in space. The study used very small tools to see details too tiny for our eyes.

questions.question: What tool did scientists use to study the tiny details?

background_read: Asteroids are rocks in space that can tell us about the early solar system. Bennu is a carbon-rich asteroid, meaning it has carbon-based materials similar to those needed for life. NASA's OSIRIS-REx mission collected samples from Bennu and brought them to Earth in 2023. Scientists study these samples to learn how water and chemicals interacted billions of years ago. This helps us understand where life's ingredients might come from in space.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from NASA and researchers like Mehmet Yesiltas
WHAT: Found three different chemical spots in asteroid Bennu showing uneven water effects
WHERE: In samples from asteroid Bennu, studied on Earth
WHY: To learn about the early solar system and ingredients for life

perspectives.description: Shows how water created a patchwork of chemicals in Bennu, preserving life's ingredients.
